aximar.title: 3D Plotting
aximar.description: Interactive 3D plots: surfaces, parametric curves, implicit surfaces, contours, and vector fields.

cells.source:
# 3D Plotting

`ax_draw3d` produces interactive 3D plots you can rotate, zoom, and hover over. It supports surfaces, parametric curves, parametric surfaces, implicit surfaces, contour projections, scatter data, and vector fields.

## Surfaces

`explicit(expr, x, xlo, xhi, y, ylo, yhi)` renders z = f(x,y). Drag to orbit, scroll to zoom.

ax_draw3d(
  explicit(sin(x)*cos(y), x, -%pi, %pi, y, -%pi, %pi),
  title="sin(x)·cos(y)",
  xlabel="x", ylabel="y", zlabel="z"
)
Use `colorscale` to change the colour palette.

ax_draw3d(
  explicit(x^2 - y^2, x, -2, 2, y, -2, 2),
  colorscale="Viridis",
  title="Saddle: x² − y²"
)

## Parametric Curves

`parametric(x(t), y(t), z(t), t, t₀, t₁)` traces a space curve.

ax_draw3d(
  color="crimson", line_width=3,
  parametric(cos(t), sin(t), t/(2*%pi), t, 0, 4*%pi),
  title="Helix"
)
A trefoil knot — increase `nticks` for smoother curves.

ax_draw3d(
  color="teal", line_width=3, nticks=1000,
  parametric(
    sin(t) + 2*sin(2*t),
    cos(t) - 2*cos(2*t),
    -sin(3*t),
    t, 0, 2*%pi
  ),
  title="Trefoil Knot"
)

## Parametric Surfaces

`parametric_surface(x(u,v), y(u,v), z(u,v), u, u₀, u₁, v, v₀, v₁)` builds surfaces that can't be expressed as z = f(x,y) — spheres, tori, and more.

ax_draw3d(
  parametric_surface(
    cos(u)*sin(v), sin(u)*sin(v), cos(v),
    u, 0, 2*%pi, v, 0, %pi
  ),
  colorscale="Blues",
  title="Sphere"
)
A torus — the outer radius is 3, the tube radius is 1.

ax_draw3d(
  parametric_surface(
    (3 + cos(v))*cos(u),
    (3 + cos(v))*sin(u),
    sin(v),
    u, 0, 2*%pi, v, 0, 2*%pi
  ),
  colorscale="Viridis",
  title="Torus"
)

## Implicit Surfaces

`implicit(eqn, x, x₀, x₁, y, y₀, y₁, z, z₀, z₁)` renders the isosurface f(x,y,z) = 0. This is slower than explicit surfaces (default 20³ = 8 000 evaluations) but handles any equation. Use `nticks=N` to control the grid resolution.

ax_draw3d(
  implicit(
    x^2 + y^2 + z^2 = 1,
    x, -1.5, 1.5, y, -1.5, 1.5, z, -1.5, 1.5
  ),
  title="Unit Sphere (implicit)"
)
A genus-2 surface — try rotating it to see the holes.

## Surface Contours

`ax_contour3d` renders z = f(x,y) as a surface with contour lines projected below — a useful way to see level sets in 3D.

ax_draw3d(
  ax_contour3d(sin(x)*cos(y), x, -%pi, %pi, y, -%pi, %pi),
  colorscale="Viridis",
  title="sin(x)·cos(y) with contour projection"
)

## 3D Vector Fields

`ax_vector_field3d(Fx, Fy, Fz, x, x₀, x₁, y, y₀, y₁, z, z₀, z₁)` renders a vector field as cones. The default grid is 8³ = 512 cones.

ax_draw3d(
  ax_vector_field3d(-y, x, 0, x, -2, 2, y, -2, 2, z, -1, 1),
  colorscale="Blues",
  title="Rotation Field: F = (−y, x, 0)"
)
A radial field — cones point outwards from the origin.

ax_draw3d(
  ax_vector_field3d(x, y, z, x, -1, 1, y, -1, 1, z, -1, 1),
  colorscale="Hot",
  title="Radial Field: F = (x, y, z)"
)
## 3D Scatter Data

ax_draw3d(
  marker_size=5, color="darkorange",
  points([[1,1,1],[2,2,8],[3,3,27],[4,4,64],[5,5,125]]),
  title="Cubic Growth"
)

## Combining Objects

Mix different 3D types in a single plot — here a surface with a parametric curve lying on it.

ax_draw3d(
  opacity=0.6,
  explicit(sin(x)*cos(y), x, -%pi, %pi, y, -%pi, %pi),
  color="red", line_width=4,
  parametric(t, t, sin(t)*cos(t), t, -%pi, %pi),
  title="Curve on Surface"
)

## Tips

- **Rotate**: drag to orbit the view
- **Zoom**: scroll wheel
- **Pan**: ctrl+drag (or right-drag)
- **Hover**: hover over surfaces/cones to read coordinates
- Use `nticks=N` to control grid resolution (default 50 for surfaces, 20 for implicit, 500 for curves)
- Use `ngrid=N` for vector field density (default 8 — keep ≤ 15 for performance)
- Use `opacity` < 1 to see through overlapping surfaces
